div.h3: Why the Wrong Clock Breaks Profiling

g.text: Concrete Example: read() Blocked on Disk I/O

ul.li: CLOCK_REALTIME → "What time is it?" → Use for user-visible timestamps
CLOCK_MONOTONIC → "How long did this take?" → Always use for duration measurement

div.p: clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts) Kernel maps a read-only page into every process's address space. Contains pre-computed time values updated by kernel timer interrupts. No ring-0 transition required — pure userspace memory read. Cost: ~5–20 ns per call (vs ~100 ns for a real syscall)

text.tspan: blocks in kernel waiting for disk

div.p: timespec_diff_ns(&entry_time, &exit_time) = 300,000,000 ns → reported as 300ms Accurate because disk I/O wait (300ms) completely dominates tracer overhead (~2–10µs context switches). NTP step of ±50ms would corrupt CLOCK_REALTIME result.
